title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh, 42, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the New England Patriots Foundation.
McHugh began running marathons to honor her late uncle, a retired fire chief who lost his battle with cancer.
After surviving Stage 3 breast cancer, McHugh overcame treatment challenges to prepare for her next marathon.

title: Markey presses Meta on facial recognition risks in smart glasses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Senator Ed Markey questions Meta's potential facial recognition tech in smart glasses, citing privacy risks.
Markey, Wyden, and Merkley demand Meta disclose plans for facial recognition by April 6.
Concerns include identification at protests and potential risk to privacy and civil liberties.

title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy City Council delays a $2.65 million plan for PFAS-free firefighter gear, frustrating union leaders.
Mayor Thomas Koch seeks funding to replace gear after tests found harmful PFAS chemicals in current equipment.
Council members cite concerns about cost and durability of PFAS-free gear, delaying the decision for further review.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Mayor Cathleen DeSimone proposed transforming Attleboro's Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve to cut long-term expenses.
The zoo needs $57 million in capital improvements and costs $900,000 annually to operate, straining the city's budget.
Zoo officials prioritize animal care but encourage public input as the proposal progresses; some raised concerns about the plan.

title: What does it mean to have a partner?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Watertown couple Colin Wong and Abigail Brown held a last-minute microwedding in Somerville after a blizzard disrupted their plans.
FX's 'Love Story' prompts nostalgia for 1990s communication as portrayed in the lives of John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette.
Debate arises over the use of 'partner' in relationships, questioning if it's used prematurely in new or long-distance dating.

title: After loss and cancer, a Burlington runner keeps moving toward Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Adam Averback, a Burlington resident, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team MGH after surviving testicular cancer.
Averback's father died from glioblastoma; both received treatment at Massachusetts General Hospital, which he honors by participating.
Despite a serious blood infection, Averback resumed training, aiming to complete the marathon as a tribute to endurance and survival.

title: New Patriots S Kevin Byard working out with rumored New England trade target A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots show interest in Eagles' WR A.J. Brown but offer was declined by Philadelphia.
Brown and new Patriots safety Kevin Byard have been working out together, fueling trade speculation.
Eagles might trade Brown after June 1 to save cap space, seeking a first-round draft pick.

title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens completed his sophomore season at Boston College with 47 points in 34 games.
Hagens is expected to sign an entry-level contract with the Bruins, potentially joining them for their final regular-season games.
The Bruins, after minimal trade deadline action, see Hagens as a key addition for their playoff push.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Is This Thing On?' directed by Bradley Cooper, stars Will Arnett as a stand-up comic and is streaming on Disney+.
'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' continues Tommy Shelby's story during WWII and is available on Netflix.
'Born to Bowl' is a new docuseries about professional bowling, narrated by Liev Schreiber, streaming on HBO Max.

title: ‘We’ll see’: Alex Cora mum on future with Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora is entering his eighth season as Red Sox manager, with his contract running through 2027.
Cora is not revealing plans beyond 2027, emphasizing his family's happiness and the team's current state.
Cora is 134 wins away from surpassing Terry Francona for second-most managerial wins in Red Sox history.
